root_text.an10.167:1.1: Tena kho pana samayena jāṇussoṇi brāhmaṇo tadahuposathe sīsaṁnhāto navaṁ khomayugaṁ nivattho allakusamuṭṭhiṁ ādāya bhagavato avidūre ekamantaṁ ṭhito hoti.
root_text.an10.167:2.1: Addasā kho bhagavā jāṇussoṇiṁ brāhmaṇaṁ tadahuposathe sīsaṁnhātaṁ navaṁ khomayugaṁ nivatthaṁ allakusamuṭṭhiṁ ādāya ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ.
root_text.an10.167:2.3: “kiṁ nu tvaṁ, brāhmaṇa, tadahuposathe sīsaṁnhāto navaṁ khomayugaṁ nivattho allakusamuṭṭhiṁ ādāya ekamantaṁ ṭhito?
root_text.an10.167:3.1: “Yathā kathaṁ pana, brāhmaṇa, brāhmaṇānaṁ paccorohaṇī hotī”ti?
root_text.an10.167:3.2: “Idha, bho gotama, brāhmaṇā tadahuposathe sīsaṁnhātā navaṁ khomayugaṁ nivatthā allena gomayena pathaviṁ opuñjitvā haritehi kusehi pattharitvā antarā ca velaṁ antarā ca agyāgāraṁ seyyaṁ kappenti.
root_text.an10.167:3.3: Te taṁ rattiṁ tikkhattuṁ paccuṭṭhāya pañjalikā aggiṁ namassanti:
root_text.an10.167:3.6: Tassā ca rattiyā accayena paṇītena khādanīyena bhojanīyena brāhmaṇe santappenti.
root_text.an10.167:3.7: Evaṁ, bho gotama, brāhmaṇānaṁ paccorohaṇī hotī”ti.
root_text.an10.167:4.1: “Aññathā kho, brāhmaṇa, brāhmaṇānaṁ paccorohaṇī hoti, aññathā ca pana ariyassa vinaye paccorohaṇī hotī”ti.
root_text.an10.167:4.2: “Yathā kathaṁ pana, bho gotama, ariyassa vinaye paccorohaṇī hoti?
root_text.an10.167:4.3: Sādhu me bhavaṁ gotamo tathā dhammaṁ desetu yathā ariyassa vinaye paccorohaṇī hotī”ti.
root_text.an10.167:5.1: “Tena hi, brāhmaṇa, suṇāhi, sādhukaṁ manasi karohi; bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an10.167:5.2: “Evaṁ, bho”ti kho jāṇussoṇi brāhmaṇo bhagavato paccassosi.
root_text.an10.167:6.2: ‘pāṇātipātassa kho pāpako vipāko diṭṭhe ceva dhamme abhisamparāyañcā’ti.
root_text.an10.167:10.3: So iti paṭisaṅkhāya pisuṇaṁ vācaṁ pajahati;
root_text.an10.167:11.3: So iti paṭisaṅkhāya pharusaṁ vācaṁ pajahati;
root_text.an10.167:15.5: Evaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, ariyassa vinaye paccorohaṇī hotī”ti.
root_text.an10.167:16.1: “Aññathā kho, bho gotama, brāhmaṇānaṁ paccorohaṇī hoti, aññathā ca pana ariyassa vinaye paccorohaṇī hoti.
root_text.an10.167:16.2: Imissā, bho gotama, ariyassa vinaye paccorohaṇiyā brāhmaṇānaṁ paccorohaṇī kalaṁ nāgghati soḷasiṁ.
root_text.an10.167:16.4: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.an10.167:1.1: Now, at that time it was the sabbath. The brahmin Jānussoṇi had bathed his head and dressed in a new pair of linen robes. Holding a handful of fresh grass, he stood to one side not far from the Buddha.
translation_text.an10.167:2.3: “Brahmin, why have you bathed your head and dressed in a new pair of linen robes? Why are you standing to one side holding a handful of fresh grass?
translation_text.an10.167:2.4: What’s going on today with the brahmin clan?”
translation_text.an10.167:2.5: “Worthy Gotama, today is the ceremony of descent for the brahmin clan.”
translation_text.an10.167:3.1: “But how do the brahmins observe the ceremony of descent?”
translation_text.an10.167:3.2: “Well, worthy Gotama, on the sabbath the brahmins bathe their heads and dress in a new pair of linen robes. They make a heap of fresh cow dung and spread it with green grass. Then they make their beds between the boundary and the fire chamber.
translation_text.an10.167:3.3: That night they rise three times and worship the fire with joined palms:
translation_text.an10.167:3.5: And they serve the fire with abundant ghee, oil, and butter.
translation_text.an10.167:3.6: And when the night has passed they serve the brahmins with delicious fresh and cooked foods.
translation_text.an10.167:3.7: That’s how the brahmins observe the ceremony of descent.”
translation_text.an10.167:4.1: “The ceremony of descent observed by the brahmins is quite different from that observed in the training of the Noble One.”
translation_text.an10.167:4.2: “But worthy Gotama, how is the ceremony of descent observed in the training of the Noble One?
translation_text.an10.167:4.3: Worthy Gotama, please teach me this.”
translation_text.an10.167:5.1: “Well then, brahmin, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an10.167:6.2: ‘Killing living creatures has a bad result in the present life and in lives to come.’
translation_text.an10.167:6.3: Reflecting like this, they give up killing living creatures,
translation_text.an10.167:6.4: they descend from killing living creatures.
translation_text.an10.167:7.2: in the present life and in lives to come.’
translation_text.an10.167:7.3: Reflecting like this, they give up stealing,
translation_text.an10.167:8.2: in the present life and in lives to come.’
translation_text.an10.167:8.3: Reflecting like this, they give up sexual misconduct,
translation_text.an10.167:9.2: in the present life and in lives to come.’
translation_text.an10.167:9.3: Reflecting like this, they give up lying,
translation_text.an10.167:10.2: in the present life and in lives to come.’
translation_text.an10.167:10.3: Reflecting like this, they give up divisive speech,
translation_text.an10.167:11.2: in the present life and in lives to come.’
translation_text.an10.167:11.3: Reflecting like this, they give up harsh speech,
translation_text.an10.167:12.2: in the present life and in lives to come.’
translation_text.an10.167:12.3: Reflecting like this, they give up talking nonsense,
translation_text.an10.167:13.2: in the present life and in lives to come.’
translation_text.an10.167:13.3: Reflecting like this, they give up covetousness,
translation_text.an10.167:14.2: in the present life and in lives to come.’
translation_text.an10.167:14.3: Reflecting like this, they give up ill will,
translation_text.an10.167:15.2: in the present life and in lives to come.’
translation_text.an10.167:15.3: Reflecting like this, they give up wrong view,
translation_text.an10.167:15.5: This is the ceremony of descent in the training of the Noble One.”
translation_text.an10.167:16.1: “The ceremony of descent observed by the brahmins is quite different from that observed in the training of the Noble One.
translation_text.an10.167:16.2: And, worthy Gotama, the ceremony of descent observed by the brahmins is not worth a sixteenth part of the ceremony of descent observed in the training of the Noble One.
translation_text.an10.167:16.4: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
